description: Fail-loud: chronon3d_cli render must abort loudly when an image asset is structurally corrupt (e.g., truncated PNG, invalid magic bytes). Per user-spec verbatim 'PNG inesistente/corrotto', this fixture extends the Test #7 fail-loud contract to the IMAGE-decode stage. Canonical error vocabulary: Composition/Layer/Asset/Path tokens in stderr + ImageDecoder-specific context, NO silent fallback markers, exit code != 0, output MP4 absent, error message MUST mention the asset name + the owner layer name (per user-spec 'errore che indica asset+owner/layer').
_image_corruption_setup_hint: Pre-create /tmp/chronon_corrupt_image_harness/corrupt.png with a deliberately truncated PNG header (e.g., zero-byte file or partial 8-byte signature) before invoking the CLI. The harness directory is supplied; the rotting byte stream is provided externally to keep the JSON declarative.
honesty_note: Per AGENTS.md §honesty: the corruption-byte-stream harness is EXTERNAL to this JSON (because binary fixtures do not belong in tracked JSON). The harness directory is created in the bash script's setup phase if absent; the CLI invocation may then fall back to AssetResolver-failure if the harness is unprepared — the assertion is the SAME (fail-loud, not the specific rot trigger). Per AGENTS.md Cat-3 anti-duplication: this JSON schema is shared across all 5 Test #7 + 6 NEW asset preflight fixtures, with the rot_class + ImageDecoder-specific token + error_must_mention differing per fixture — no duplicate schema files.
